The April 25, 2016 trial date is advanced to April 4, 2016 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Preference status remains, as does the 120th day of April 28, 2016. All provisions of the January 5, 2016 order granting preference remain in place, except for the following amendments: Motions for summary judgment/adjudication have already been filed. The last day to hear such motions is March 29, 2016, with oppositions due 10 days before the hearing and replies 4 days before the hearing. The Court will allow a maximum of three summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Prior to the hearing on this motion, plaintiff has met and conferred with all defendants that have filed motions for summary judgment/adjudication and at the hearing must new hearing dates proposed. Refer to signed order for dates of the new dates of the summary motion hearings. Fact discovery cut-off date is April 1, 2016. Expert discovery remains open until the case is assigned out to a trial department. Expert designations to be filed/served by March 4, 2016. The parties are ordered to provide final confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. Plaintiff shall provide final confidential demands to Pang Ly by March 7, 2016 at 5:00p.m. Defendants shall provide their final confidential offers to Pang Ly by March 14, 2016 by 5:00 p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00a.m. on March 30, 2016. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rules 5 and 20 on April 4, 2016 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jan-05-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Patrick Burke

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

No objection made to the tentative ruling, and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial date is April 25, 2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. The 120th day is April 28, 2016 (as stated in this Court's tentative ruling posted January 29, 2015, if the motion is granted, the 120th day will be calculated based on the scheduled hearing date of December 30, 2015). The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 14, 2016. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than February 26, 2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 11, 2016. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by April 18, 2016 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00a.m. on April 21, 2016. Fact discovery cut-off date is April 8, 2016. Expert discovery cut-off date is April 22, 2016. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than January 29, 2016. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than January 15, 2016 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than January 15, 2016 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on February 17, 2016 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on April 25, 2016 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by April 21, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kimberly R. Hendershott of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12552 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
